blockers.title: Rate limit is enforced after Convex Auth has already replaced the magic-link code
blockers.evidence: `convex/auth.ts:78-87` puts `enforceSendLimits` inside `sendVerificationRequest`. In the pinned Convex Auth implementation, `signIn.js:62-79` calls `callCreateVerificationCode` before `sendVerificationRequest`, and `createVerificationCode.js:42-57` deletes the existing code and inserts the new one. So an over-limit request can still invalidate a real member's already-emailed link without sending a new email. That leaves a targeted sign-in denial path and does not deliver SEC-2's protective choke point.
blockers.required_fix: Move the send limit check before any `authVerificationCodes` create/replace side effect, or otherwise guarantee over-limit attempts do not create, delete, or replace verification codes. Add a regression test for: valid link requested, over-limit request follows, original link still works.

blockers.title: Minor and unknown-age pipeline consent can still be written during public join
blockers.evidence: SEC-5 says pipeline consent for `minor` and `restricted_unknown` must be refused, with no consent row written. `writeConsent` has that guard, but the public `submitJoin` path passes `args.consents` into `createPendingMember` (`convex/members.ts:237-244`), and `createPendingMember` unconditionally writes the pipeline consent value (`convex/members.ts:292-296`) after deriving the lane. A 15-year-old who checks the pipeline box gets a `pipeline=true` consent row. Vault source says minors are blocked from the talent pipeline (`01 Under-18 Members & Mentorship Safeguards`, lines 47-50; Privacy decision, lines 38-39).
blockers.required_fix: Apply the lane guard to the join/internal consent write path too. For minor or restricted_unknown lanes, reject or force `pipeline=false` with an auditable refusal, and add tests covering `submitJoin` with minor DOB + `pipeline=true` and no-DOB/restricted_unknown + `pipeline=true`.

blockers.title: DOB hardening still accepts under-13 self-members
blockers.evidence: The vault locks minimum joining age 13 and says under-13s are not signed up as their own members (`01 Under-18 Members & Mentorship Safeguards`, lines 37-38 and 76-78; Privacy decision, line 74). The new `isValidDob` only checks ISO format, real date, not future, and year >= 1900 (`convex/lib/age.ts:24-37`). `submitJoin` accepts any DOB that passes that check (`convex/members.ts:230-232`), so a child under 13 can create an account and reach the logged-in pending-guardian portal state.
blockers.required_fix: Reject DOBs that make the applicant under 13 at the public join boundary before inserting any member or consent rows. Add unit coverage for under-13 rejection and 13th-birthday acceptance.

non_blocking_notes: `npm test` could not be executed in this read-only review sandbox because Vitest tried to write `node_modules/.vite-temp/...` and failed with EPERM. This is an environment limitation, not a passing test result.

Added member-facing strings for the rate-limit and pending-review states are plain language and contain no em dashes. The diff does add em dashes in code comments, which is not member-facing but still worth cleaning because the repo brand lock is strict.

`public/_headers` ships the requested header families, but the CSP uses broad `*.convex.cloud` / `*.convex.site` instead of a single deployment origin. Tighten when the production Convex origin is fixed.

spec_compliance.criterion: SEC-1 DOB required at public join boundary
spec_compliance.evidence: Missing/malformed DOB is rejected by `submitJoin`, but the boundary does not enforce the vault's 13+ minimum, and `createPendingMember` still has a generic optional DOB path that creates `source: "new_signup"` rather than an explicit migration-only path.
`beforeSessionCreation` maps `minor` to `pending_guardian`, `restricted_unknown` to `pending_review`, and issues certificates only when `next === "active"` (`convex/auth.ts:128-150`).

spec_compliance.criterion: SEC-2 rate limiting on magic-link sends
spec_compliance.evidence: Limits exist, but they run in `sendVerificationRequest` after Convex Auth has already replaced the stored verification code, so over-limit calls still mutate auth state and can invalidate legitimate links.
`updateProfile` checks `_storage` metadata, allows only jpeg/png/webp, enforces <= 5 MB, and returns before patching on invalid photo (`convex/members.ts:178-187`).
`writeConsent` refuses pipeline opt-in for minor/restricted_unknown, but public join bypasses that guard and can write `pipeline=true` through `createPendingMember`.
`public/_headers` includes CSP plus `nosniff`, `DENY`, `strict-origin-when-cross-origin`, and a minimal permissions policy; CSP breadth noted separately.
`package.json` pins `@convex-dev/auth` to exact `0.0.94`, and `convex/auth.ts:1-3` records why.

Vitest suite is committed for age, member lane, lifecycle, profile validation/completeness, and rate-limit window logic with more than 19 test cases. Execution was blocked by read-only sandbox writes.

spec_compliance.criterion: QA-2 CI runs type gate and unit tests
spec_compliance.evidence: `.github/workflows/ci.yml:28-38` runs `npm run check`, `npm test`, then the E2E suite.
No new pages or design rewrites were added; UI changes are limited to handling the new hardening states/errors.

summary: Do not ship. The diff closes some obvious holes, but the rate limiter is placed too late to protect magic-link integrity, the join path can still store forbidden pipeline consent for minors, and the touched DOB boundary still permits under-13 self-members against the vault.
